methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $81M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Zeta Surgical

data.narrative:
Wispr AI is a Series A biotech and health company founded by Tanay Kothari that has raised $81M to date. The valuation analysis applies two triangulation methods across a peer universe of 11 comparable companies: 3 public-market comps (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-stage peers in biotech and health, including Eikon Therapeutics, Xaira Therapeutics, Neko Health, Asimov, and New Limit.

The Stage-Progression method applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the $81M raised proxy, yielding $162M. The Peer-Median method anchors to a cohort of 7 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $810M and a range spanning $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (sector median 6.1x) cannot be applied, as Wispr's revenue remains undisclosed—standard for private companies at this stage.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $138M (low) to $931M (high), with a median of $486M. This wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around Wispr's revenue generation, market traction, and competitive positioning relative to the peer set. The $162M stage-progression floor provides a conservative anchor; the $810M peer median suggests upside if Wispr's clinical or commercial metrics exceed the cohort average. Key uncertainties include undisclosed revenue, burn rate, and the specific therapeutic or diagnostic application driving comparability assumptions.
